(Amended) Notice Of Motion To Set Aside The Default And Default Judgment Against Williams And Burrows Inc.
Off Calendar Feb-07-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Stephen E. Carlson of IMAI, TADLOCK, KEENEY & CORDERY, LLP (415-675-7000), appearing for moving parties: specially appearing Hartford Fire Insurance Company and Nationwide Insurance Co. as alleged insurers of defendant Williams and Burrows Inc.

Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Robert Ross (274099), Michael Steinberger (274642), Willie Howard (275138), Monroe Amey (275535) and Joan Valladon as wrongful death heir and as successor-in-interest to George Valladon, deceased, et al. (421945).

RULING:
SPECIALLY APPEARING HARTFORD FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY, NATIONWIDE INSURANCE CO.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST WILLIAMS AND BURROWS INC.

On the record the parties stipulated to argue all the motions in the following cases at the same hearing: Robert Ross (274099), Michael Steinberger (274642), Willie Howard (275138), Monroe Amey (275535) and George Valladon (421945).

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Specially appearing Hartford Fire Insurance Company and Nationwide Insurance Co.'s motion to set aside the default and default judgment entered against defendant Williams & Burrows, Inc. is granted in part and denied in part. Hartford Fire Insurance Company and Nationwide Insurance Co. demonstrated that the Court should invoke its inherent, equitable power to set aside a default judgment on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake because they demonstrated a meritorious defense, a satisfactory excuse for not presenting a defense to the original action, and diligence in seeking to set aside the default judgment once it was discovered. Therefore the default judgment entered against Williams & Burrows, Inc. on July 28, 2015 is set aside solely as related to Hartford Fire Insurance Company and Nationwide Insurance Co. The motion to set aside the entry of default is denied. With the default judgment set aside, it is unnecessary to set aside the entry of default because an insured's default has no effect on the insurer(s). (Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Janice H. Dispo of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13299 (Tel: 510-451-1580). (503/CML)
